_note: Queen reads this to recommend models for hardware. Autoresearch loop updates it.

nano.note: Pi, thin client. Qwen 2.5 1.5B is current best at this size.

edge.note: Old laptop, Chromebook. Qwen 2.5 3B beats phi4-mini on most benchmarks.

worker.note: Desktop, gaming PC. Qwen 2.5 7B is top tier at this size.

heavy.note: Workstation, Mac Studio. Qwen 2.5 32B for deep reasoning.

beast.note: Multi-GPU server, 128GB+. Qwen 2.5 72B is frontier-class local.

nvidia_cuda.note: NVIDIA GPUs get Nemotron for function calling + Qwen coder. CUDA optimized.

apple_metal.note: MLX backend. Unified memory = larger models fit. Qwen 2.5 runs well on Metal.

rockchip_npu.note: Use rkllama for NPU inference. Qwen 2.5 1.5B confirmed on RK3588 NPU.

architecture_notes.qwen2.5: Current best general-purpose family. Tops benchmarks at every size (1.5B, 3B, 7B, 32B, 72B). Strong code, math, multilingual. March 2026.
architecture_notes.nemotron: NVIDIA-optimized. Best for function calling on CUDA hardware. 4B params.
architecture_notes.gemma3: Google. Strong instruction following. 27B is the sweet spot for deep synthesis.
architecture_notes.phi4-mini: Microsoft. 3.8B. Good reasoning per param but being surpassed by Qwen 2.5 3B.
architecture_notes.qwen2.5-coder: Code-specific variant. 7B and 14B. Top ranked for code gen March 2026.

thin_client.note: Ghost worker + knowledge node. Not for inference.

mining_p106.note: Cheapest CUDA drone. No display output.

mining_rtx3070.note: Best mainstream mining GPU for inference.

steam_deck.note: Works if you have one. Don't buy one for this.

synology_nas.note: Already on. Free always-on node via Docker.

chromebook.note: Crostini Linux. CPU only. If you have one.

rack_server.note: Dual Xeon. Only way to run 70B under $300.
